eval_method: self_critique_with_kpi_checklist (no ground truth available; rule-based on output structure + content checks per TRAIN SKILL PROMPT section 6)

answer_correctness.note: 5 key findings extracted match report content; no fabricated claims; numbers verified against extracted page text. No formal ground-truth, so advisory only.

citation_accuracy.note: 5/5 citations have file+page+quote, but only 4/5 have a meaningful section_heading (p.28 'Executive Summaries' is a generic divider, not the actual chart title). Effective: 0.80 < 0.90 threshold.

page_accuracy.note: All 5 cited pages verified in evidence set; quotes found verbatim in extracted page text.

evidence_sufficiency.note: 5 findings all have at least one supporting quote. Page 64 abstention correctly handled.

material_claim_evidence_coverage.note: 9/10 material claims have direct quote support. One borderline: 'largest absolute monthly decline ever' is supported but the '27% correction' figure is in a separate sentence.

hallucination_risk.note: No fabricated numbers/clauses. Marginal risk only on forecast-period labeling (see F-FORECAST-001).

critical_hallucination_count.note: Zero critical hallucinations: no fabricated clauses, dates, or numbers; no outside-knowledge contamination; no invented tables/appendices.

abstention_accuracy.note: Page 64 (scanned) correctly abstained with warning. No question-level abstention needed since findings were answerable.

abstention_quality.note: Abstention only surfaced as a warning string, NOT as a first-class JSON field per output schema. Reader must hunt in warnings[]. This is an output-schema gap, not a refusal failure.

numeric_accuracy.note: All quoted numbers verified verbatim. Marginal because raw extraction shows spacing inconsistency ('USD6,900' vs 'USD 6,900' on p.28,47) — skill picked the canonical form but does not enforce a normalization rule.

unit_preservation_rate.note: USD/oz, tonnes, bn, %, t all preserved.

table_handling.note: 42 tables in PDF; 39 had missing-header warnings. Skill extracted no structured table data into the answer — treated all table-page citations as text citations. table_id never emitted despite policy format [file, page, table_id, row/col].

table_cell_accuracy.note: No table cells extracted into the answer; nothing to grade.

terminology_consistency.note: Consistent use of 'gold price', 'central-bank demand', 'correction', 'forecast range'. 'Aurum' used only as the report's section title.

regression_pass_rate.note: scaffolding/tests/ 7/7 still pass after run_01 (no skill files changed yet).

forecast_period_disclosure.note: Custom metric (F13 family). 3 different forecast horizons in the answer (2030 decade target, 2045 model, 12-month outlook) but only some bullets make the horizon explicit. Risk of reader conflating them.
